optionA.text: External segmentation and setae absent

optionB.text: External segmentation and/or setae present

optionA.text: Paired antennae on the prostomium

optionB.text: Paired antennae absent; tactile hairs along the body; small interstitial forms

optionA.text: Body long and slender; interstitial forms

optionB.text: Body short and saclike, pelagic

optionA.text: Body a flattened disc with indistinct segmentation; ectoparasitic

optionB.text: Body not a flattened disc; segmentation usually distinct, if indistinct, then body clearly longer than wide

optionA.text: Dorsum with series of elytra (scales) or distinct elytral scars present at the dorsal side of notopodial bases in several segments; felt of matted notosetae may be present

optionB.text: Dorsum without elytra, elytral scars or felt

optionA.text: Neuracicula distally hammer-headed

optionB.text: Neuracicula distally pointed

optionA.text: Prostomium with a single median antenna; dorsum with felt, or notosetae harpoon-shaped or held erect over the dorsum

optionB.text: Prostomium with one to three antennae; dorsum without felt; notosetae usually distinctly lateral in position, never harpoon-shaped

optionA.text: Neurosetae composite

optionB.text: Neurosetae simple

optionA.text: All posterior segments with elytra; prostomium with one to three antennae

optionB.text: Elytra alternate with dorsal cirri along the whole length of the body; one antenna present

optionA.text: Spinning glands present; median antenna, if present, attached near the posterior or middle of the prostomium; notosetae absent

optionB.text: Spinning glands absent; median antenna attached at the anterior margin of the prostomium; notosetae usually present

optionA.text: Notopodia with expanded, golden or brassy setae that more or less cover the dorsum

optionB.text: Notosetae otherwise (may be absent)

optionA.text: Prostomium with large facial tubercle and a median antenna; notosetae in rosettes

optionB.text: Prostomium without a facial tubercle; paired lateral and a median antenna present; notosetae in transverse rows

optionA.text: Posterior end covered ventrally by a chitinized shield

optionB.text: Posterior end not covered by a shield

optionA.text: Prostomium completely retracted between the first parapodia which have three pairs of tentacular cirri, partially supported by acicula

optionB.text: Prostomium not completely retracted between the first parapodia which are otherwise equipped

optionA.text: Anterior end with one or several series of long, specialized setae either covering the retractable anterior end or forming an operculum or a series of long protective spines (paleae)

optionB.text: Anterior end without exceptionally long, specialized setae (NOTE: Short, strong hooks may be present)

optionA.text: Specialized setae long and chambered, forming a protective cage around the retractable anterior end; body with numerous epithelial papillae

optionB.text: Specialized anterior setae do not form a protective cage; anterior end not retractable; skin-papillae few and small, or absent; setae otherwise

optionA.text: Specialized setae slender, distally curved, often spinous; prostomium with seven antennae

optionB.text: Specialized setae stout, smooth and not distally curved; prostomium without appendages or with numerous tentacles

optionA.text: Specialized setae in a transverse row; tube conical, usually formed of closely fitted sandgrains

optionB.text: Specialized anterior setae either as a fan-shaped group of paleae on either side of the anterior end or as an operculum to the tube

optionA.text: Specialized setae form paleae; anterior end with two to four pairs of branchiae

optionB.text: Specialized setae form an operculum; anterior end without branchiae

optionA.text: Anterior end, including in part the prostomium, transformed into a tentacular crown

optionB.text: Anterior end not transformed into a tentacular crown (NOTE: Antennae and tentacular cirri may be crowded near the anterior end)

optionA.text: Tube calcareous; thoracic membrane present

optionB.text: Tube mucoid or horny, often covered with sand-grains; thoracal membrane absent

optionA.text: Tube irregularly twisted or straight, sometimes coiled near base; body symmetrical; more than four thoracic setigers

optionB.text: Tube completely coiled; body asymmetrical; four thoracic setigers present
optionB.result: SPIRORBIDAE (Usually considered a sub-family of the SERPULIDAE)

optionA.text: Parapodia with uncini in one or a few distinct rows; tentacular crown with smooth or pennate radioles

optionB.text: Small uncini massed in dense fields in the neuropodia only; short tentacular crown with branching tentacles

optionA.text: Digestive tract recurved with anus far anteriorly; thorax without hooks except in the first setiger

optionB.text: Digestive tract straight with far posterior or terminal anus; thorax with hooks in most setigers

optionA.text: Setiger 4 with one or a few thick spines; some median parapodia strongly modified, usually fan-shaped; tubes parchmentlike, or, if horny, distinctly annulated

optionB.text: Setiger 4 without thick spines (NOTE: Other setigers may have modified setae); no parapodia fan-shaped; tubes never parchmentlike, if horny, then without annulations

optionA.text: Numerous tentacles on the lower side of the prostomium or on the peristomium; branchiae, if present, limited to a few pairs of anterior setigers

optionB.text: Anterior end with a limited (10 or fewer pairs, usually) number of antennae and tentacular cirri, or without appendages

optionA.text: Branchiae in a transverse or oblique row or grouped in two groups on either side of the anterior dorsum, usually digitiform and smooth, more rarely bipinnate or lamellate (NOTE: Branchiae are often lost, but scars remain); buccal tentacles retractable; uncini with teeth in one or a few rows

optionB.text: Branchiae, if present, on two-three successive segments, stalked or sessile, branched or as numerous filaments, rarely smooth; buccal tentacles non-retractable; uncini with several teeth in one or more transverse rows above the main fang (crested)

optionA.text: Thoracic uncini long-handled, abdominal ones short-handled

optionB.text: Both thoracic and abdominal uncini short-handled; sometimes with a posterior prolongation in thoracic uncini

optionA.text: Prostomium with at least one pair of antennae; peristomium usually with paired palps or tentacular cirri

optionB.text: Prostomium without appendages or with a single antenna; peristomium with paired dorsal palps, maximally two pairs of tentacular cirri or without appendages

optionA.text: Prostomium continued posteriorly in a caruncle; large notosetae furcate; others smooth or serrated

optionB.text: Caruncle absent; furcate notosetae, if present, small, or furcate setae only kind of setae present

optionA.text: Notosetae arranged in transverse rows on dorsum; branchiae shorter than setae

optionB.text: Notosetae in tufts on the notopodial lobes; branchiae conspicuous branching tufts

optionA.text: Palps absent

optionB.text: Palps present, sometimes as ventrolateral pads on the peristomium or fused to the anterior end of the prostomium so that the latter appear cleft, but usually free and digitate

optionA.text: Setae absent; acicula present only in the prolonged acicular lobes of the second segment (first segment in juveniles); otherwise absent

optionB.text: Setae or acicula or both present in most segments

optionA.text: Prostomium long and conical; usually annulated, with two pairs of antennae at the tip

optionB.text: Prostomium no more than twice as long as wide, never annulated; antennae long or short

optionA.text: Eversible pharynx with four jaws in a cross; parapodia either all uniramous or all biramous

optionB.text: Eversible pharynx with more than four jaws; parapodia uniramous anteriorly and biramous posteriorly

optionA.text: Jaws present

optionB.text: Jaws absent

optionA.text: Each jaws consisting of a series of denticles in a row

optionB.text: Each jaw consisting of a single piece

optionA.text: Four or five pairs of jaws

optionB.text: A single pair of jaws present

optionA.text: Both composite and simple setae present, parasitic in decapod crustaceans

optionB.text: All setae composite, parasitic in fish

optionA.text: One pair of antennae; interstitial forms

optionB.text: More than one pair of antennae

optionA.text: Eyes larger than the rest of the prostomium, with well defined lenses and pigment layers

optionB.text: Eyes, if present, smaller than the prostomium proper, usually small pigment cups, but lensed eyes occur

optionA.text: Series of large epithelial capsules on the dorsum; two or three pairs of lateral antennae and one median, unpaired antenna

optionB.text: Epithelial capsules absent (NOTE: Dorsal cirri of phyllodocids may be inflated, but are associated with the parapodia rather than situated on the dorsum)

optionA.text: Dorsal cirri large and foliose

optionB.text: Dorsal cirri, if present, cirriform

optionA.text: Setae absent; with a few pairs of appendages anteriorly and posteriorly, parasitic in lobsters

optionB.text: Setae present; several pairs of appendages along the body

optionA.text: All setae composite, pelagic

optionB.text: At least some setae simple, benthic or parasitic forms

optionA.text: With ciliary bands on the dorsum of each segment in addition to a large ciliary organ on the prostomium; minute interstitial forms

optionB.text: External ciliation limited mostly to small patches in sensory and respiratory organs, small or large forms

optionA.text: Five prostomial antennae, inquilines in bivalved mollusks

optionB.text: Four antennae, free-living benthic forms

optionA.text: Interramal cirri between the noto- and neuropodia in most forms, all setae simple

optionB.text: Interramal cirri absent; notosetae simple, neurosetae composite

optionA.text: Palps bi- or multiarticulated

optionB.text: Palps simple, sometimes fused to the prostomium so the latter appears cleft or forming ventrolateral pads on the peristomium

optionA.text: Palps multiarticulated; tentacular cirri absent

optionB.text: Palps biarticulated; at least one pair of tentacular cirri

optionA.text: Pharynx with paired jaws; paragnaths or soft papillae or both on the surface of the everted pharynx or pharynx smooth, parapodia usually biramous

optionB.text: Pharynx usually without jaws; paragnaths or pharyngeal papillae always absent; everted pharynx often with a circlet of distal papillae or lappets, parapodia often sub-biramous or uniramous

optionA.text: Palps ventrolateral pads on the peristomium; five occipital and two frontal antennae

optionB.text: Palps either fused anteriorly to the prostomium or as free ventrolateral projections; maximally five antennae

optionA.text: Palps free ventrolateral projections, sometimes fused to each other

optionB.text: Palps fused to the prostomium so that the latter appears anteriorly cleft

optionA.text: Prostomium longer than wide, with a pair of antennae at the tip

optionB.text: Prostomium no longer than wide (NOTE: Fused palps may make it appear longer than it is)

optionA.text: Jaws present

optionB.text: Jaws absent

optionA.text: Parapodia strongly prolonged, with long supportive acicula; anterior part of the digestive tract not visibly separated into distinct parts, pelagic

optionB.text: Parapodia not strongly prolonged; acicula short; anterior part of the digestive tract with a visible proventriculus in most species

optionA.text: Eversible pharynx, if present, unarmed

optionB.text: Eversible pharynx with four pairs of upper and one pair of lower jaws

optionA.text: Anterior end, including both pro- and peristomium without appendages (NOTE: Appendages may be present on some anterior setigers)

optionB.text: Prostomium with a single median antenna, or peristomium with paired palps or tentacular cirri or both pro- and peristomium equipped as indicated

optionA.text: Paired palps on the first or one of the first postperistomial segments

optionB.text: Paired palps absent

optionA.text: Both composite falcigers and simple setae present

optionB.text: All setae simple, distally curved or straight, sometimes acicular

optionA.text: With a single mid-dorsal palp on one of the first setigers (setigers 3-6 usually)

optionB.text: Mid-dorsal palp absent

optionA.text: With series of long, slender branchial filaments and tentacular and dorsal cirri along the body (often lost, scars remain)

optionB.text: Branchial filaments and tentacular cirri absent or limited to a few segments

optionA.text: Parapodia strongly reduced so that the setae appear sessile on the body-wall; all setae simple, true capillary setae absent

optionB.text: Parapodia usually well developed or at least present as low folds; setae usually of several different kinds, including in most cases true capillary setae

optionA.text: Thorax with series of long dorsal cirri supported by acicula

optionB.text: Body usually not clearly regionated; apart from papillae and reduced parapodia, other appendages absent

optionA.text: At least some setae with an internal structure of small chambers

optionB.text: No setae chambered

optionA.text: With a papilla between the rami of each parapodium; setae smooth and slightly recurved

optionB.text: Without papillae between the rami; setae otherwise

optionA.text: Setae in four fascicles on each segment (biramous condition)

optionB.text: Setae in two fascicles on each segment (uniramous condition)

optionA.text: Prostomium an oblique plaque, usually bordered by a flange

optionB.text: Prostomium pointed, rounded or blunt

optionA.text: Setae include anterior spines, rostrate long-shafted uncini and spinose or smooth capillaries; segments usually elongated (bamboo-worms)

optionB.text: Setae include bilimbate and spatulate kinds, long-handled uncini and companion-setae; segments not prolonged

optionA.text: Body separated into two regions with different kinds of setae in a thoracic and abdominal region (NOTE: Regions may sometimes also be definable on parapodial features)

optionB.text: Body not separated into regions; setal distribution and parapodial shapes grade along the body

optionA.text: Thorax with lateral parapodia, abdomen with both noto- and neuropodia in dorsal positions

optionB.text: Parapodia lateral in all parts of the body; notopodia often reduced in posterior segments and neuropodia may form nearly complete cinctures

optionA.text: Slender capillary setae in thorax and sometimes in the first few abdominal segments only; branchiae, if present, retractable filaments on the abdomen

optionB.text: Slender capillary setae present on anterior, median and sometimes posterior parts of the body, including the branchial region; branchiae non-retractable, bushy or simple filaments

optionA.text: Anterior end with complex jaw-apparatus

optionB.text: Jaw-apparatus absent

optionA.text: Each jaw consisting of a series of small denticles

optionB.text: Each jaw consisting of a single piece

optionA.text: Hooded hooks present in at least some setigers; one pair of maxillary carriers

optionB.text: Hooded hooks absent; three maxillary carriers

optionA.text: Branchiae present on maximally 15-20 segments, starting from one of setigers 4-10

optionB.text: Branchiae, if present, either limited to the extreme anterior end, or found scattered over a large part of the body

optionA.text: Setae include anterior spines, rostrate long-shafted uncini and spinose or smooth capillaries; segments usually elongated (bamboo-worms)

optionB.text: Setal distribution otherwise; segments rarely elongated

optionA.text: Setae include simple capillaries and simple bifid falcigers in both noto- and neuropodia in a long region of the body

optionB.text: Simple bifid falcigers absent

optionA.text: Prostomium an elongated cone, usually more than twice as long as wide, nearly always articulated

optionB.text: Prostomium less than twice as long as wide, may be bluntly conical, rounded or truncate, never articulated

optionA.text: Eversible pharynx with four jaws; parapodia either all uniramous or all biramous

optionB.text: Eversible pharynx with more than four jaws; anterior parapodia uniramous, posterior ones biramous

optionA.text: Parapodia rounded lobes with large, easily dehiscent cirri; pelagic

optionB.text: Parapodia low folds or blunt, button-shaped projections; cirri, if present, cirriform

optionA.text: Composite falcigers present in anterior setigers

optionB.text: All setae simple

optionA.text: Neurosetae uncinal in structure

optionB.text: Uncini absent

optionA.text: All setae simple capillaries; branchiae cirriform, pectinate or absent; prostomium entire, pointed or rounded

optionB.text: Furcate and acicular setae usually present; branchiae, if present, limited to the anterior end and strongly arborescent; prostomium T-shaped or bifid

optionA.text: Prostomium with a median antenna

optionB.text: Prostomium without appendages

optionA.text: Branchiae present on maximally 15-20 segments first starting from one of setigers 4-10

optionB.text: Branchial distribution otherwise or branchiae absent

optionA.text: Notopodial cirri flask-shaped in some setigers, plumose setae present

optionB.text: Notopodial cirri cirriform or foliose, plumose setae absent

optionA.text: With two pairs of tentacular cirri; setae composite

optionB.text: Without tentacular cirri; setae simple

optionA.text: Setal appendages long and slender; pelagic

optionB.text: Setal appendages short; parasitic

optionA.text: With multiple series of small, long-shafted uncini in the neuropodia

optionB.text: Uncini in single rows or absent

optionA.text: Prostomium flattened and spatulate; as wide as the widest part of the body

optionB.text: Prostomium not flattened; distinctly narrower than the widest part of the body

optionA.text: Parapodia inconspicuous; abdominal segments prolonged with setae forming complete cinctures around the body

optionB.text: Parapodia well developed; abdominal segments usually not prolonged; setae limited to lateral tufts

optionA.text: All parapodia biramous, except possibly the first one

optionB.text: Median parapodia uniramous

optionA.text: Uncini absent; notopodia with acicula, but without setae; true branchiae absent

optionB.text: Uncini normally present, at least in the neuropodia; notopodia with setae in addition to acicula; branchiae present or absent
